text:
In a sleepy town nestled between whispering woods and tranquil rivers, lived a lonesome figure known by the locals only as Mister Silent. People whispered rumors that he carried an enigma with him; one whisper said he spoke only the common tongue of gravel, while another told of eerie silence that swallowed entire conversations.

It was on a particularly frosty autumn evening that Mister Silent set forth from his solitary abode to undertake the annual harvest festival. His silence was unnerving; in every step he took, the crunch of pine needles beneath his soft boots was the only sound. As he approached the festival grounds, a fire flickered in his eyes, though his mouth remained ever closed.

Nervous nods, knowing looks, people clearing their throats—all went unheeded by Mister Silent. He stopped before the grand oak, setting up his stall with the precision of a maestro conducting a silent symphony. He was selling silence, a rare commodity in this lively town.

The townsfolk gathered
